description: A 'thereo' is a third person who helps people in disagreement hear one another and seek a settlement they can choose themselves. The mediator supports communication and may clarify what remains apart. The role carries no power to impose an outcome.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and release into true-mid front 'e'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue tip before stressed true-mid front 'e'. Keep final true-mid rounded 'o' separate in hiatus.
sound_symbolism: Dental breath in 'the' meets the tongue turn of stressed 're' before the final rounded vowel opens on its own. The pauses between contacts give the form room to settle without collapsing its separate sounds.
usage_notes: The noun names the person in the role. The people, matter, procedure, confidentiality, and possible outcomes belong in the surrounding account. 'thorae' (facilitator) supports a group process more generally, while a mediator works between stated parties whose positions remain their own.

examples.translation: The mediator listens to the people.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A mediator attends to several conditioned accounts without turning any speaker into a fixed enemy or pretending that every disagreement has a middle point.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Mediation keeps the people in a disagreement as authors of any settlement while giving careful listening and clarification an explicit role.

semantic_domains.communication: helping stated parties listen, clarify, and seek their own settlement
semantic_domains.community: a third-party role used when a shared relation needs mediated dialogue
